completion_promises.criterion_text: src/jarvis/tools/__init__.py (or wherever assemble_tool_list lives from FEAT-J002) gains an include_frontier: bool = True keyword-only parameter.
completion_promises.evidence: Added `*, include_frontier: bool = True` keyword-only parameter to assemble_tool_list in src/jarvis/tools/__init__.py. Verified by tests TestAC001IncludeFrontierKeywordOnly: parameter exists, default is True, kind is KEYWORD_ONLY, annotation is bool, positional pass raises TypeError.

completion_promises.criterion_text: When include_frontier=True: the returned list includes escalate_to_frontier alongside all FEAT-J002 tools.
completion_promises.evidence: Imported escalate_to_frontier from jarvis.tools.dispatch and conditionally include it in the returned list when include_frontier=True. Tests TestAC002IncludeFrontierTrue verify the escalation is present, list length is 10, all FEAT-J002 tools are present, and the order is alphabetical with escalate_to_frontier between dispatch_by_capability and get_calendar_events.

completion_promises.criterion_text: When include_frontier=False: the returned list omits escalate_to_frontier entirely and includes ALL other FEAT-J002 tools unchanged.
completion_promises.evidence: When include_frontier=False the function returns the original FEAT-J002 9-tool list verbatim. Tests TestAC003IncludeFrontierFalse verify escalate_to_frontier is absent (by name and by object identity), length is 9, and the names match FEAT_J002_TOOL_NAMES exactly.

completion_promises.criterion_text: The returned list is a new list object each call (no mutable aliasing); the reasoning model cannot mutate a shared list to add escalate_to_frontier at runtime (ADR-ARCH-023).
completion_promises.evidence: Both branches of the conditional return a fresh list literal on each call. Tests TestAC004NoMutableAliasing verify two consecutive calls return distinct list instances, attended and ambient calls return distinct instances, and a hostile append of escalate_to_frontier to a returned ambient list does not leak into a subsequent ambient call.

completion_promises.criterion_text: No default coupling: include_frontier has no relationship to session state; it is a pure lifecycle-time flag set by the caller (lifecycle.startup in TASK-J003-015).
completion_promises.evidence: The function never reads dispatch._current_session_hook or dispatch._async_subagent_frame_hook. Tests TestAC005NoSessionCoupling verify the flag is honoured both with no session resolver wired (True and False cases) and even when a fake attended session resolver is installed — the flag's value alone determines the surface.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Identity check: escalate_to_frontier in assemble_tool_list(..., include_frontier=False) is False; ... is True when include_frontier=True.
completion_promises.evidence: Tests TestAC006IdentityCheck use the imported escalate_to_frontier object (not just the string name) and assert membership matches the flag exactly: `escalate_to_frontier in result` is True when include_frontier=True and False when include_frontier=False.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: Code follows the existing module patterns: PEP 8 / Black-compatible formatting, type hints, comprehensive docstrings, and from __future__ import annotations. The new test file mirrors the conventions of tests/test_assemble_tool_list.py. All 196 tests across the touched modules pass with no warnings or errors attributable to this change.

test_output_summary: 23 new Layer 3 tests pass; 196 tests across all directly-related modules (assemble_tool_list, supervisor_with_tools, supervisor_lifecycle_wiring, escalate_to_frontier layers 1+2, import_graph, reserved_packages) pass. 4 failing tests in the full suite are pre-existing and unrelated (verified by stashing this branch's changes and re-running): they are subprocess `import jarvis` env-setup failures and an unrelated get_calendar_events docstring whitespace mismatch.
implementation_notes: Layer 3 of the DDR-014 belt+braces gate. Added include_frontier: bool = True keyword-only parameter to jarvis.tools.assemble_tool_list. The default-True attended surface splices the FEAT-J003 escalate_to_frontier between dispatch_by_capability and get_calendar_events alphabetically; include_frontier=False returns the original FEAT-J002 9-tool surface verbatim. Two separate list literals (rather than copy-and-remove) make the surface obvious and avoid any shared mutable state. The imported escalate_to_frontier was also added to __all__ as part of the dispatch group (no other production module relied on its absence). Existing tests test_assemble_tool_list.py and test_supervisor_with_tools.py were updated to reflect the new attended default (10 tools, EXPECTED_TOOL_ORDER includes escalate_to_frontier) — this is a contract-level update following from AC-001 mandating default=True. The Layer 3 gate is constitutionally not reasoning-adjustable (ADR-ARCH-023): the model cannot invoke a tool it cannot see, and the per-call fresh list literal means a hostile mutation cannot leak between calls.
requirements_addressed: AC-001 keyword-only include_frontier parameter with default True
AC-002 attended surface contains escalate_to_frontier plus all FEAT-J002 tools
AC-003 ambient surface omits escalate_to_frontier and keeps every FEAT-J002 tool unchanged
AC-004 fresh list per call — no mutable aliasing
AC-005 no session-state coupling — pure lifecycle-time flag
AC-006 identity check via the actual tool object
